questions.specification: In a mechanical system, which scenario represents increased speed due to mechanical transmission?

options.content: A small gear turning a large gear
options.markscheme: This configuration results in a speed reduction and an increase in torque.
A flywheel stores rotational energy to smooth out power delivery; it is not a mechanism for increasing speed through gear transmission.

options.content: A large gear turning a smaller gear
options.markscheme: When a larger gear (the driver) turns a smaller gear (the driven), the output speed increases because the smaller gear must rotate more times for every single rotation of the larger gear.
Friction acts to oppose motion, resulting in a reduction of speed and loss of energy as heat.

questions.specification:
What is this image an example of?

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/notes/28f7c530-00f3-4df7-901a-56453946c59f.jpeg)

options.markscheme: This image displays a gear system. When a larger driver gear is used to turn a smaller driven gear, the output rotational speed increases. This is a common application of gear ratios in mechanical systems.
Resonance refers to the reinforcement or prolongation of sound or vibration, which is not represented by a mechanical gear assembly.
Gears can be used to increase torque when a smaller driver gear turns a larger driven gear, but the arrangement shown is an example typically associated with increasing speed (large to small).

options.content: Changing the direction of rotation in a gear train
options.markscheme: Meshing gears can reverse direction, but the key feature illustrated is the gear ratio effect (speed increase) rather than direction change.

questions.specification:
What type of system is depicted here?

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/notes/1ae49717-88d2-4e52-8fa4-6e48e808b442.jpeg)

options.markscheme: Incorrect. A chain-driven system uses sprockets and a metal chain to transmit motion, whereas the image shows pulleys and a belt.
Incorrect. In a direct drive system, the power source is connected directly to the output without intermediate belts or pulleys.
Correct. The system shown uses a flexible belt and pulleys to transmit power and motion between shafts.
Incorrect. A pneumatic system uses compressed air and pneumatic cylinders or motors to generate motion.

questions.specification: A linkage is used in a mechanical toy to change the motion from circular to up-and-down. This demonstrates which type of motion conversion?
Which component converts rotary motion into reciprocating motion?
19. Which feature most influences the direction of motion in a cam system?

Which class of lever is shown in this diagram?

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXfUNv48-8oSxHgK9kkaFZXGu28LaTD1GxwddTACqviWttKnUhCFfqZYEk7ays9_Jkt7JEXgP6UE1av2n2i0i1NfnwT_Ebj8gKse5rgvtycUha7hQGFrwX0DTFt2xXrF7Jfw1v-9FA?key=gz2IFjHMQBPKFRswYwU8xQ)

options.markscheme: Not a standard lever class; levers are typically classified as first-, second-, or third-class.

questions.specification: A company is designing a height-adjustable standing fan for home and office use. They want to ensure users can easily control the fan's direction, height, and speed using simple mechanical systems. The design must be cost-effective, user-friendly, and reliable.

parts.content:
**Figure 1**: A standing fan with a telescopic pole and height-adjustment


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/cfc5fed0-308c-47d8-bc0c-5ee89d1cff99)

Identify the type of motion created when adjusting the height of the fan.
parts.markscheme: - Linear motion. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: State one reason why telescopic poles are used in height adjustment.
parts.markscheme: - Allows compact storage and adjustable height in a single structure. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content:
**Figure 2**: Close-up of the fan’s oscillation mechanism using a crank and gear system


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/ece2f39e-1580-47bd-8d91-13e8cc100d12)

Outline how the crank and gear mechanism produces oscillating motion.
parts.markscheme:
- Crank converts rotary motion from the motor into reciprocal motion. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Gear transfers this movement to rotate the fan head side to side. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Describe one benefit and one drawback of using this mechanical oscillation system.
parts.markscheme:
- Benefit: Provides wide air coverage without user effort. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Drawback: More mechanical parts increase the chance of wear. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content:
**Figure 3:** Control panel showing a rotary dial for adjusting fan speed 


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/2eb06b30-d28c-4b16-8fcb-2344b17711b0)

Explain three advantages of using a rotary dial over a digital button interface.
parts.markscheme:
- More intuitive for users to turn and adjust speed levels. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Lower production cost due to simpler components. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Works without software or electronics. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Discuss how using mechanical systems in this fan supports sustainable design.
parts.markscheme:
- Reduces reliance on electronic components, making recycling easier. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Easier to repair and maintain, encouraging long-term use. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Consumes less energy compared to digital alternatives. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Fewer electronic waste components generated at end-of-life. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Simpler disassembly improves material separation and reuse. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Durability of mechanical systems extends product lifespan. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Compare the three types of levers and their use in mechanical systems.
parts.markscheme:
- 1st class lever: The fulcrum ($F$) is located between the effort ($E$) and the load ($L$) (e.g., $L-F-E$). <marks>A1</marks>
- 2nd class lever: The load ($L$) is located between the fulcrum ($F$) and the effort ($E$) (e.g., $F-L-E$). <marks>A1</marks>
- 3rd class lever: The effort ($E$) is located between the fulcrum ($F$) and the load ($L$) (e.g., $F-E-L$). <marks>A1</marks>
- 1st class use: Can act as either a force multiplier or a speed/distance multiplier depending on the relative proximity of the fulcrum to the load or effort. <marks>A1</marks>
- 2nd class use: Always acts as a force multiplier because the effort arm is always longer than the load arm, resulting in a Mechanical Advantage ($MA$) $> 1$. <marks>A1</marks>
- 3rd class use: Acts as a speed or distance multiplier; while it requires more effort than the weight of the load ($MA < 1$), the load moves a greater distance. <marks>A1</marks>
- 1st class example: e.g. seesaw, crowbar, or scissors. <marks>A1</marks>
- 2nd class example: e.g. wheelbarrow, nutcracker, or bottle opener. <marks>A1</marks>
- 3rd class example: e.g. tweezers, fishing rod, or tongs. <marks>A1</marks>

Award [1] for each correct description of position, use/evaluation, and example for each of the three lever types, up to [9 marks].

parts.content: Identify two components in a mechanical system that convert input into output.
parts.markscheme:
- Identify one correct component, for example gears <marks>A1</marks>
- Identify a second correct component, for example pulleys <marks>A1</marks>

2 marks total

<note>Award [1] for each correctly named component up to [2]. Other acceptable examples include levers, cams, linkages, or belts.</note>

questions.specification:
A scissor jack is used to lift a vehicle, as shown in the diagram. Which of the following best describes the mechanical advantage provided by this machine?

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/notes/cf85dcfb-eb51-4851-a05f-0b3b29a2f391.jpeg)

options.content: It reduces the total work required to lift the vehicle.
options.markscheme: Simple machines do not reduce the total work required to complete a task; they only change the magnitude or direction of the forces involved. In real systems, friction actually increases the total work input required compared to lifting the object directly.

options.content: It allows a smaller input force to lift a larger output load.
options.markscheme: A mechanical advantage greater than 1 ($MA > 1$) indicates that the machine is a force multiplier. This allows the user to apply a smaller input force (effort) over a larger distance to move a larger output force (load) over a smaller distance. <marks>A1</marks>

options.content: It increases the vertical displacement of the load relative to the input displacement.
options.markscheme: In a force multiplier, the input distance must be greater than the output distance to satisfy the principle of conservation of energy ($F_{in}d_{in} \geq F_{out}d_{out}$). Therefore, the load moves a smaller distance than the input movement.

options.content: It increases the efficiency of the energy transfer to the car to over 100%.
options.markscheme: Efficiency is the ratio of useful work output to total work input. Due to energy losses (primarily friction in the screw threads), the efficiency of a jack is always less than 100%.

questions.specification: Which mechanical system best increases torque while reducing output speed?

options.content: Small gear driving a large gear
options.markscheme: In a gear train, when a smaller gear (the driver) turns a larger gear (the driven), the gear ratio is greater than 1. This configuration provides a mechanical advantage, increasing the output torque while simultaneously reducing the output rotational speed. <marks>A1</marks>

While a pulley system can change speed and torque, the statement is generic. Without specifying that a small pulley is driving a large pulley, it does not inherently describe a torque-increasing system as clearly as option A.
A lever with a central fulcrum (Class 1 lever with equal arms) has a mechanical advantage of 1. It changes the direction of force but does not increase torque or change speed ratios.

A camshaft and follower are used to convert rotary motion into reciprocating linear motion. While the profile of the cam determines the timing and displacement, it is not primarily a system for rotary torque amplification.

labeledSubSubTopics.path: A. Design in theory > A3.3 Introduction to mechanical systems (HL only) > A3.3.1 Types of motion
labeledSubSubTopics.title: A3.3.1 Types of motion
labeledSubSubTopics.reasoning: The question specifically asks for the definition and an example of linear motion, which falls directly under the 'Types of motion' sub-topic within mechanical systems.

parts.content: Define linear motion and give an example of a product that uses it.
parts.markscheme:
- Linear motion is movement in one direction along a straight line <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Example: a paper trimmer or a sliding drawer <marks>1 mark</marks>

questions.specification: Which of the following devices uses rotary motion to produce intermittent output motion?

What is being transmitted through this gear system?

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/notes/f62e7472-fa7a-4c45-9806-d15d06268bc2.jpeg)

options.markscheme: Incorrect. A gear system is a mechanical transmission system, not an electrical one.
Incorrect. While friction may generate minor heat, the primary purpose of gears is the transmission of motion, not energy dissipation.
Correct. Gears are mechanical components used to transmit rotary motion and torque between shafts.
Incorrect. Fluid pressure is the medium of transmission in hydraulic systems, whereas gears use mechanical contact.

questions.specification: Which type of motion involves movement back and forth in a curved path?
Which best describes the function of linkages in mechanical systems?

options.content: To increase friction in rotating shafts
options.markscheme: Incorrect. Linkages are designed to transmit motion and force efficiently; increasing friction is usually undesirable in such mechanisms.
Incorrect. While linkages can be part of a larger system that manages acceleration, their primary definition is the transformation of motion and force.

options.content: To redirect or modify the magnitude of force and motion
options.markscheme: Correct. Linkages are mechanical assemblies designed to convert one type of motion into another (e.g., rotary to linear) or to provide mechanical advantage by modifying force.

options.content: To change the voltage of a power system
options.markscheme: Incorrect. Voltage regulation is an electrical function performed by transformers, not a mechanical linkage.

questions.specification: A team is analyzing bicycle drivetrain and gear-shifting systems to demonstrate mechanical motion, force amplification, and control.

parts.content:
**Figure 1** shows the bicycle rear derailleur and sprocket cluster.

![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/9efb3621-d922-4bd5-ad2d-acb8325fa7d5)

Identify the type of motion of the rear derailleur pulley.
parts.markscheme: - The pulley rotates on its axle to guide the chain (rotary motion). <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Explain how the derailleur maintains the correct chain tension.
parts.markscheme:
- Spring-loaded arm applies tension to the lower pulley. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Moves to take up slack as chain shifts between gears. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Keeps chain aligned and prevents skipping or derailing. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content:
**Figure 2** shows the front chainring with crank and pedal assembly.


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/c79f437f-a07e-40c6-9cff-9f8165096906)

Explain one advantage of having multiple chainrings or gears on the crankset.
parts.markscheme:
- Allows changing mechanical advantage (easier pedaling uphill or faster on flats). <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Enables efficient power transfer by keeping cadence in optimal range. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Refer to **Figures 1 & 2**. Evaluate how gear systems on a bicycle demonstrate mechanical efficiency and energy use.
parts.markscheme:
- Multiple gears allow efficient energy conversion across varied terrains. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Low gears amplify torque for climbing, reducing rider fatigue. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- High gears increase speed efficiently on flat terrain. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Derailleur reduces friction and chain drag compared to hub gears. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Efficient chain transfers over 95% of pedal power. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Regular maintenance (lubrication/alignment) necessary to preserve efficiency. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content:
**Figure 3** shows the brake lever and cable-pull mechanism on the handlebar.


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/69b93e52-457e-4dc8-86f3-d49b4ccd8842)

Describe how the brake lever converts user input into braking force at the wheel.
parts.markscheme:
- User squeezes lever → pulls cable. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Cable tension actuates brake caliper or mechanical actuator. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Leverage at the hinge amplifies force applied at the cable. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Caliper mechanism presses brake pads (friction surfaces) against the rim or rotor to decelerate the wheel. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Refer to **all three figures**. Compare mechanical vs hydraulic/electronic alternatives in bicycles (e.g., hydraulic brakes, electronic shifting).
parts.markscheme:
- Mechanical gears/brakes are reliable and easy to repair. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Hydraulic/electronic systems offer smoother actuation and precision. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Mechanical systems have lower initial cost and require no batteries. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Hydraulic brakes deliver greater stopping power with self-adjustment. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Electronic shifting ensures precise gear changes under load. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Electronic systems are heavier and complex to maintain in the field. <marks>1 mark</marks>

questions.specification: Which factor determines the classification of a lever?

options.content: The material used in the beam
The position of the fulcrum relative to effort and load
The distance moved by the load

parts.content: Explain how the four types of motion can be combined to form a complex mechanical system.
parts.markscheme:
- **Describe** how rotary motion from a motor drives a gear <marks>1 mark</marks>
- **Explain** how a gear connects to a cam to produce reciprocating motion <marks>1 mark</marks>
- **Outline** how a cam connects to a lever to create oscillating motion <marks>1 mark</marks>
- **State** how the system can include a belt drive to transfer rotary motion between shafts <marks>1 mark</marks>

questions.specification: Which best describes a purpose of a reverse motion linkage?

parts.content: Describe how mechanical systems are used to modify motion.
parts.markscheme:
- Gear systems can increase speed by using a larger driving gear to turn a smaller driven gear. <marks>A1</marks>
- Belt and pulley systems can decrease speed by using a smaller driving pulley to turn a larger driven pulley. <marks>A1</marks>
- Cams and followers can convert rotary motion into reciprocating or oscillating motion. <marks>A1</marks>
- Levers can increase the magnitude (distance) of motion by using a longer output arm relative to the input arm. <marks>A1</marks>

*Award [1] for each correct description of a component's effect on motion, up to [4 max].*

**[4 marks]**

questions.specification: This question explores the relationship between cam profiles and the resulting mechanical motion of the follower, as well as their practical applications in engineering and manufacturing.

parts.content: Discuss how different cam shapes produce distinct types of motion and their uses.
parts.markscheme:
- **Eccentric (circular) cam**: produces a smooth reciprocating motion (simple harmonic motion) in the follower. <marks>A1</marks>
- **Application**: used in fuel pumps or simple mechanical toys to create smooth, continuous back-and-forth movement. <marks>A1</marks>
- **Pear-shaped cam**: produces a motion characterized by a dwell period where the follower remains stationary for approximately half of the rotation. <marks>A1</marks>
- **Application**: used in internal combustion engines to control the timing and duration of valve opening/closing. <marks>A1</marks>
- **Snail cam**: produces a gradual rise followed by an immediate, sudden drop. <marks>A1</marks>
- **Application**: used in stamping machines or clock strike mechanisms where a quick release or impact is required. <marks>A1</marks>

*Award [1] for each correctly identified motion and [1] for each corresponding application, up to a maximum of [6].*

questions.specification: What happens when gears with a different number of teeth engage?

options.content: The gear speed and torque change

questions.specification: A startup is developing a foldable laptop stand aimed at remote workers. The stand must be lightweight, structurally stable, and easy to manufacture. The team is analysing different components to ensure structural integrity, user comfort, and manufacturability.

parts.content:
**Figure 1** shows the full laptop stand in an open position


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/568ed8e5-39ce-45e6-84b6-6ddfaa127388)

Identify the type of structural system formed by the bracing in **Figure 1**.
parts.markscheme: - Triangulated frame system <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Explain two benefits of using a frame in a structure like **Figure 1**.
parts.markscheme:
- Triangulated frames provide high rigidity and resistance to deformation under load. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- They distribute forces efficiently through axial members. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content:
**Figure 2** shows the material used for the frame, which is a thin aluminium alloy.


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/6b9514d9-d081-4380-aeb9-4e186d974abf)

With reference to **Figure 2**, describe three reasons why aluminium is an appropriate material for the laptop stand.
parts.markscheme:
- It is lightweight, making the stand highly portable. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- It has good corrosion resistance, ensuring durability even in humid environments. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- It can be easily extruded into complex cross-sections for structural efficiency. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content:
**Figure 3** shows the folding hinge mechanism of the stand that allows it to collapse flat for storage.


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/d684b07e-034f-4285-b817-1da4caf32160)

Suggest one structural challenge related to the hinge mechanism in **Figure 3** and propose a solution.
parts.markscheme:
- Challenge: The hinge could become loose or misaligned over time, reducing stability. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Solution: Use a locking mechanism or friction washers to maintain consistent alignment. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: With reference to **Figures 1-3**, analyse how the structural design of the stand balances strength with portability.
parts.markscheme:
- Use of hollow aluminium reduces weight while retaining strength due to its geometry. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Foldable joints and minimal material use enhance portability. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Triangulated geometry ensures load distribution, preventing wobbling or collapse. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- The structure must avoid over-engineering to maintain user-friendliness and low cost. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Evaluate the suitability of aluminium compared to injection-moulded plastic for this application.
parts.markscheme:
- Aluminium provides high strength-to-weight ratio and structural rigidity. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- It is more durable for repeated folding and load-bearing use. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- However, aluminium is costlier and less forgiving in terms of tactile comfort. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Plastic may offer better aesthetics and can integrate hinges in a single mould. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- But plastics may deform or fatigue more quickly under stress. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- The choice depends on whether the priority is long-term durability or low-cost manufacturing. <marks>1 mark</marks>

questions.specification:
What type of motion is being demonstrated in this image?

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/notes/56815e79-b1aa-4dfc-899e-3add851e2fe3.jpeg)
Which class of lever has the load between the effort and fulcrum?

parts.content: Outline how mechanical advantage is achieved using a lever system.
parts.markscheme:
- **State** that mechanical advantage is achieved when the effort arm is longer than the load arm; <marks>A1</marks>

- **Explain** that a longer effort arm reduces the amount of effort required to lift or move a load; <marks>A1</marks>

- **Describe** how the positioning of the fulcrum relative to the effort and load determines the lengths of the arms and thus the mechanical advantage; <marks>A1</marks>

- **Provide** a relevant example such as using a crowbar to lift a heavy object or a second-class lever; <marks>A1</marks>

4 marks total

parts.content: Explain the function of belt-driven pulley systems and their applications.
parts.markscheme:
- **Explain** that belt-driven pulley systems are used to transfer rotary motion between shafts <marks>A1</marks>
- **Explain** that they can increase or decrease output speed or torque through the use of different pulley diameters <marks>A1</marks>
- **Explain** that they enable the transmission of power over distance where gears may be impractical <marks>A1</marks>
- **Identify** a common application in domestic appliances such as washing machines <marks>A1</marks>
- **Identify** an application in household tools such as vacuum cleaners <marks>A1</marks>
- **State** that the system typically consists of a driver pulley, a driven pulley, and a flexible belt <marks>A1</marks>

6 marks total

<note>Award [1] for each accurate point or example, up to [6 max].</note>

questions.specification:
## Question
Which feature of gears allows precise motion control and force transmission?

options.content: The number and shape of teeth
options.markscheme: Gears transmit motion and force through interlocking teeth, which prevents slipping and allows for a constant velocity ratio and precise control over the gear ratio.

options.content: The surface roughness of the gear teeth

questions.specification:
Consider this bicycle gear system. What best describes the function of this mechanical system?

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXdMpAD13lnmNLNGuit7GK36w2g2QEt9I6utKWpJ5TBumGfYbAOzCX63-87Z21dXi02iBgR5BzP_iiweXEQTsSWYuBdyIDp1Xfcf019aSlrs0l1q69TFYv5enr-5akt1osSVpLbCkg?key=gz2IFjHMQBPKFRswYwU8xQ)

options.content: Converts rotational speed into increased torque at the wheel (mechanical advantage)
options.markscheme: Incorrect: while gears can change torque and speed, the overall function is broader than only increasing torque (it can increase or decrease torque depending on the gear ratio).

options.content: Converts a user input into a controlled motion output at the wheel
options.markscheme: Correct: the gear system transmits and modifies the rider's pedalling input to produce controlled rotational motion at the wheel (via torque/speed changes).

options.content: Changes rotational speed and torque by varying the gear ratio (velocity ratio)
options.markscheme: Incorrect: this describes an effect/characteristic of gearing rather than the best overall function description in general terms for the system.

options.content: Stores mechanical energy and releases it to smooth motion
options.markscheme: Incorrect: a bicycle gear system does not primarily store energy; it transmits and transforms motion/torque via gear ratios.

questions.specification: Which of the following is a dynamic force acting on a mechanical system?

options.markscheme: This is a static force because it does not vary with time while the object remains stationary.

options.content: Normal reaction force from a stationary floor
options.markscheme: This is a static force as it remains constant for an object at rest.

options.content: Wind load on a moving bridge
options.markscheme: Wind load is a dynamic force because it varies in magnitude and direction over time and its effects are influenced by the motion of the structure.

options.content: Impact force during a hammer strike
options.markscheme: An impact force is dynamic because it changes rapidly with time; therefore this is not a correct option for a static force.

questions.specification: An engineering company is developing a low-cost, hand-powered mechanical toy that performs multiple movements when a crank is turned. The team integrates cams, linkages, and combined motion systems to create dynamic movement in the toy figure for both educational and entertainment purposes.

parts.content:
**Figure 1** shows a pear-shaped cam and follower system connected to a rotating handle.


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/113dae0d-0341-4c5b-92af-b719808efbf1)

Identify the type of motion generated by the cam in **Figure 1** and name the cam profile.
parts.markscheme:
Pear cam generates reciprocating or oscillating motion. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] for stating “oscillating” or “reciprocating” motion and “pear cam” as the profile.*

parts.content: Explain how using cams of different profiles would affect the motion of the toy figure in **Figure 1**.
parts.markscheme:
Different cam profiles produce different types of motion. <marks>1 mark</marks>

They vary in dwell time and rate of acceleration. <marks>1 mark</marks>

This affects the realism or timing of the toy’s movement. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] for different cams changing motion type, [1] for varying acceleration or dwell periods, and [1] for describing how this changes timing or realism of movement.*

parts.content:
**Figure 2** shows a bell crank linkage used to redirect vertical motion into horizontal motion.

![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/f388901e-2090-441b-ad06-2cbb011199b1)

Describe how the bell crank linkage in **Figure 2** changes the direction of movement.
parts.markscheme:
The bell crank linkage redirects motion by 90 degrees. <marks>1 mark</marks>

It changes vertical motion into horizontal motion (or vice versa). <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] for explaining that it redirects motion by 90 degrees and [1] for noting the change from vertical to horizontal (or vice versa).*

parts.content:
**Figure 3** shows a wooden automata toy with a crank mechanism combining rotary and oscillating motion.

![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/6bfb97c4-d360-49a6-bd58-a022d25b54f4)

Outline how rotary motion is converted into two different types of motion in the automata toy shown in **Figure 3**.
parts.markscheme:
The crank produces rotary motion. <marks>1 mark</marks>

The cam converts rotary motion into oscillating or reciprocating motion. <marks>1 mark</marks>

The linkage transmits that motion to other moving parts. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] for crank producing rotary motion, [1] for cam converting to reciprocating/oscillating, and [1] for linkage enabling simultaneous movement.*

parts.content: Using all three figures, discuss how combining mechanical systems allows the toy to simulate complex lifelike movement.
parts.markscheme:
Rotary motion is generated by the crank mechanism. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Cams convert rotary motion into linear or oscillating motion. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Linkages transmit and change the direction of this motion. <marks>1 mark</marks>

The combined system allows multiple movements to occur together. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] for identifying combined rotary, oscillating, and reciprocating motion, [1] for cams controlling timing, [1] for linkages transmitting force, and [1] for overall effect of coordinated movement.*

parts.content: Evaluate the advantages and limitations of using combined cam and linkage systems in mechanical toys designed for children.
parts.markscheme:
Cam and linkage systems are simple and low-cost to manufacture. <marks>1 mark</marks>

They make toys engaging and visually interesting through movement. <marks>1 mark</marks>

They do not require electricity or batteries to operate. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Small mechanical parts can be fragile and may break under stress. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Movement is limited compared to digital or electronic toys. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Still suitable for educational toys or promoting sustainability. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] for simplicity of design, [1] for engaging movement, [1] for low energy requirement, [1] for fragility of small components, [1] for limited range of motion without electronics, and [1] for suitability in education or sustainability-focused toys.*

questions.specification: A crankshaft converting up-and-down piston movement into rotary motion is an example of:

A household appliance company is developing a compact hand mixer for home bakers. The mixer must be lightweight, easy to control, and capable of handling a range of mixing tasks through simple mechanical systems.

parts.content:
To address speed control ergonomics, the company is looking towards developing a hand mixer with a sliding speed control switch.

![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/62ca8df9-a03a-475a-9767-72a4b1dae0d8)

i.) Identify the type of mechanical system used in the speed control.
parts.markscheme: - Sliding lever mechanism <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: ii.) Outline one reason why sliding controls are preferred for speed settings on handheld appliances.
parts.markscheme: - They allow quick, continuous adjustment with one hand during operation <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content:
To optimise mixing efficiency, the design team analysed the beater system shown in **Figure 2**.


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/3a8406be-1a98-4011-a2c2-7899134113ee)

i.) Describe how the beaters rotate in opposite directions using a simple mechanical system.
parts.markscheme:
- Each beater is connected to gears that interlock <marks>1 mark</marks>
- When one beater spins clockwise, the other spins counterclockwise due to the gear configuration <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: ii.) Explain one advantage and one limitation of using interlocking gears in the beater mechanism.
parts.markscheme:
- Advantage: Ensures synchronized rotation for effective mixing <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Limitation: Gears may wear out quickly with dense mixtures <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content:
To ensure compact and safe power transmission, the internal gearing setup in Figure 3 is being considered.


![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/f942c9c1-413d-4ed7-b142-f15175a36a85)

Justify three reasons why worm gears are used in this type of device.
parts.markscheme:
- They reduce the high-speed rotation of the motor into low-speed, high-torque motion <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Worm gears prevent back-driving, maintaining position when not powered <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Compact design suits small handheld appliances <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Discuss how the integration of mechanical systems in **Figures 1-3** supports the functionality and safety of the hand mixer.
parts.markscheme:
- Gear reduction ensures beaters operate at safe, usable speeds <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Mechanical systems provide reliable and predictable performance <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Back-driving resistance from the worm gear increases user safety <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Physical controls (like sliders) reduce software/electronic failure risk <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Interlocking gears ensure even mixing, improving user experience <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Simplicity of mechanical systems reduces maintenance needs <marks>1 mark</marks>

questions.specification: How does a lever-based bottle opener provide mechanical advantage?

options.content: By amplifying the input force using distance from the fulcrum
By using hydraulics to apply pressure
By removing the fulcrum from the system

questions.specification: Why do belt-driven systems sometimes slip compared to gear systems?

options.content: They are made of harder materials
They rely on friction, not interlocking parts
They convert rotary to reciprocating motion

questions.specification: Which statement about belt-driven systems is true?

options.content: They are more efficient than gear systems
options.markscheme: Gears are generally more efficient than belt drives because they provide a positive drive without the energy loss associated with belt slippage and friction.
Belt-driven systems are specifically designed to transmit rotary motion from one shaft to another using a flexible belt and pulleys.

options.content: They use pulleys to transmit motion
options.markscheme: A belt-driven system consists of a flexible belt looped over two or more pulleys to transmit power and motion between shafts.

options.content: They require regular lubrication between the belt and pulley
options.markscheme: Belts typically rely on friction to transmit motion; applying lubrication between the belt and pulley would cause excessive slippage and system failure.

questions.specification:
The following diagram shows a windscreen wiper linkage system. What does this linkage system help accomplish?

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/notes/971b9ca5-3db8-462e-9dad-3cdb1461f156.jpeg)

options.markscheme: A linkage system primarily transfers and converts motion; torque increase would be achieved by gears or a different transmission, not the linkage itself.
While the motor in the system converts electrical energy to mechanical energy, the linkage system itself is designed to transform the type of motion.

options.content: Converts rotary motion to oscillating motion
options.markscheme: The linkage mechanism takes the continuous 360-degree rotary motion from the drive motor and converts it into the back-and-forth oscillating motion (moving in an arc) required for the wiper blades.

options.content: Converts rotary motion to reciprocating motion
options.markscheme: Reciprocating motion is linear back-and-forth motion; wiper blades move in an arc, so the linkage produces oscillating motion rather than reciprocating motion.

questions.specification:
What does the profile of this cam determine?

![Cam Profile](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/notes/1ec25fd1-7635-480f-9879-07a51c9dc26a.jpeg)

options.content: The input torque required to turn the cam
The material of the cam or follower
The rotational speed of the camshaft

questions.specification: Which best illustrates a mechanism that converts **reciprocating** motion into **rotary** motion?

options.content: An engine piston turning a crankshaft
options.markscheme: Piston provides reciprocating motion that is converted to rotary motion at the crankshaft (crank-slider mechanism).
Converts rotary motion to linear motion (or linear to rotary), but the linear motion is not inherently reciprocating.
Pulley motion is rotary only; it does not create reciprocating motion.
Transmits rotary motion to rotary motion; no reciprocating motion is involved.

questions.specification: Which of the following is NOT one of the four types of motion found in mechanical systems?

What type of motion conversion is shown in this rack-and-pinion system when the **pinion (gear) is the input** (turned by a handle or motor)?

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXdqV5yhpmO-sjPCypH9JIDYvE3A3NQisSrXJkHoWZ3gv5SKVPucU852LuNPiWYS3t7pDiW5Z4cEStbqcBa55Yo3lc0tNC-NXmyyQUmzbtnP0-Oeb89tsH-fCSOFTSuQB1Wm03Cw?key=gz2IFjHMQBPKFRswYwU8xQ)

options.markscheme: If the rack is the input, the motion conversion could be linear to rotary, but the question specifies the pinion is the input.
A rack-and-pinion does not inherently require reciprocating input; with pinion input it converts rotary to linear.
The system shown is not an oscillating mechanism; with pinion input it produces linear rack motion.
Turning the pinion (rotary input) drives the rack in a straight line (linear output).

topic.title: A3.3 Introduction to mechanical systems (HL only)
